trade.summary: Germania is among Norvegia's most important trading partners. Norvegia's exports to Germania are dominated by natural gas, oil, fish, and aluminum; Germania exports machinery, vehicles, and chemicals. Germania-Norvegia energy trade intensified massively after 2022 as Germania sought to replace Russian gas.

diplomatic.status: Close partners, NATO allies, EEA link
diplomatic.summary: Germania and Norvegia have excellent diplomatic relations. Norvegia is in the European Economic Area (EEA), giving it deep ties to EU regulatory frameworks while not being an EU member. Both are NATO members. Norvegia has become Germania's most critical energy partner since 2022 as Germania divested from Russian gas. Cooperation on Arctic policy, fisheries, and climate.

military.summary: Both NATO members with very close military cooperation. Germania participates in NATO's Enhanced Forward Presence in Norvegia's vicinity and cooperates on high north security. Joint exercises including Cold Response. Norvegia's geographic position is critical to NATO's northern flank.

financial.summary: Norvegia's Government Pension Fund Global (GPFG) holds significant German equity and debt. German FDI in Norvegia focuses on energy and maritime sectors. Norwegian sovereign wealth fund investment in Germania is substantial.

energy.summary: Norvegia became Germania's largest natural gas supplier after Germania's 2022 phase-out of Russian gas. Norwegian gas via North Sea pipelines (including the recently expanded capacity) is critical to Germania's energy security. Germania also imports Norwegian hydropower indirectly. Offshore wind cooperation in the North Sea is growing rapidly.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific collaboration through European research programs (Norvegia participates in Horizon Europe). Joint research in ocean science, climate, fisheries, and energy technology. Academic exchanges are significant.
